summary: Tested async import-censo (bug 520) + btn-no-changes visibility (bug 2) + regressions. All targeted fixes are working. Backend 8/9 test cases passing; 1 failure is due to Emergent LLM budget exhausted (not a code bug). Frontend btn-no-changes confirmed visible, emerald-styled, placed below btn-generate-pase.

minor.issue: Job fails with 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 1.01, Max budget: 1.0' from litellm/emergentintegrations. This blocks any real import until the LLM key budget is topped up. NOT a code bug - endpoint returned in <1s and error propagated correctly to job.status='failed' as designed.

verified_fixes: Bug 1a: POST /api/patients/import-censo returned {job_id, status:'running'} in <1s (well under 5s Cloudflare threshold).
Bug 1b: GET /api/patients/import-censo/status/{job_id} returns running->failed/done shape correctly. Structure includes new, updated, discharged, errors, parsed_count.
Bug 1d: daily_entries preserved after patient moved to discharged (test_history_preserved_on_discharge PASSED).
Bug 1e: GET /api/patients/discharged returns list with active=false ordered by discharged_at desc.
Bug 1g: 400 returned for empty .docx and non-.docx uploads; failed GPT job populates status='failed' + error field.
Bug 2: btn-no-changes visible on PatientDetail with text '✨ Paciente sin cambios · Generar todo', emerald border/bg classes, positioned below btn-generate-pase (Y 1251 > 1183).
Regression: /auth/me, /patients, /patients/discharged, /generate/no-changes all reachable and behave correctly.
action_items: HIGH: Top up / rotate the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY - budget is exhausted (Current cost: 1.01 / Max 1.0). Any live census import or GPT-5.2 generation (pase, note, no-changes) will fail with 'Budget has been exceeded' until this is resolved. This is likely the root cause of user's remaining friction, not any code bug.
critical_code_review_comments: IMPORT_JOBS is an in-memory dict (server.py:735) - jobs are lost on backend restart. Acceptable for single-user preview, but consider persisting to Mongo for reliability.
No cleanup/TTL for IMPORT_JOBS - dict grows unbounded across imports. Add expiry after N hours.
server.py is 1230 lines - approaching the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting patients / import / generation into modules.

success_rate.backend: 89% (8/9 - failure due to LLM budget, not code)

seed_data_creation: Test created + cleaned up temp patients (PACIENTE PRUEBA UNO/DOS/HISTORIAL); readmitted any real patients accidentally discharged during import. Real user data (37 patients) preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both bug fixes are correctly implemented. The blocking issue for end-to-end validation is the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exhaustion - resolve that before re-testing full GPT-parsed import flow.
